verbatim_text: Okay, so let me start a bit with myself. So I grew up in the province of China. And Confucius, Yu was one of our ancestors. And Confucius, people tell me that, my teachers tell me at least, that he invented the idea of tests or exams. And perhaps as a consequence, our province is really, really big, really serious about tests or exams. And over here it says at the level of natural secrets. So look, these armed guards are trying to move the tests that will be a matter to the students. And because the tests were so protected and there was just so much to study in my province, when studying I always have to guess the test problem. Basically, when American kids are betting on baseball, they'd be betting on the test problems. When I'm testing, I look at the test, I'm always praying that the next test problem is similar to something that I have studied. I came to America when I was 13, and I had a great discovery. America does take-home tests. And what's great about take-home tests is that you can learn the relevant content while you are taking the test. There's no guesswork.
